audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for livestock pestilence, boils, hail, Goshen distinction, Pharaoh's confession and renewed hardening.

verses.restored: YHWH said to Moses, Come to Pharaoh and speak to him: Thus says YHWH, Divine of Hebrews: Send my people away, so they may serve/worship me.
For if you refuse to send and still hold them,
look, hand of YHWH will be upon your livestock in field, upon horses, donkeys, camels, herd, and flock, a very heavy pestilence.
YHWH will make distinction between livestock of Israel and livestock of Egypt, and nothing shall die from all belonging to children of Israel.
YHWH set an appointed time, saying, Tomorrow YHWH will do this thing in the land.
YHWH did this thing next day, and all livestock of Egypt died, but from livestock of children of Israel not one died.
Pharaoh sent, and look, not even one from livestock of Israel had died; but heart of Pharaoh was heavy, and he did not send the people.
YHWH said to Moses and Aaron, Take for yourselves handfuls of soot from kiln, and Moses shall throw it toward heavens before eyes of Pharaoh.
It shall become dust over all land of Egypt, and it shall become boils breaking out in sores on human and beast in all land of Egypt.
They took soot from kiln and stood before Pharaoh, and Moses threw it toward heavens, and it became boils breaking out in sores on human and beast.
The magicians could not stand before Moses because of boils, for boils were on magicians and on all Egypt.
YHWH strengthened/hardened heart of Pharaoh, and he did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken to Moses.
YHWH said to Moses, Rise early in morning and stand before Pharaoh, and say to him: Thus says YHWH, Divine of Hebrews: Send my people away, so they may serve/worship me.
For this time I am sending all my plagues to your heart, and against your servants and your people, so that you may know there is none like me in all earth.
For now I could have stretched my hand and struck you and your people with pestilence, and you would be cut off from earth.
But for this I have made you stand: to show you my power and so my name may be recounted in all earth.
Still you exalt yourself against my people, not sending them away.
Look, tomorrow about this time I will rain very heavy hail, such as has not been in Egypt from day it was founded until now.
Now send, bring your livestock and all you have in field into safety. Every human and beast found in field and not brought into house—the hail shall come down upon them, and they shall die.
The one fearing/revering word of YHWH among servants of Pharaoh made his servants and livestock flee into houses.
But the one who did not set heart to word of YHWH left servants and livestock in field.
YHWH said to Moses, Stretch your hand toward heavens, so there may be hail in all land of Egypt, upon human, beast, and every plant of field in land of Egypt.
Moses stretched his staff toward heavens, and YHWH gave thunder and hail, and fire went earthward; YHWH rained hail upon land of Egypt.
There was hail and fire flashing within hail, very heavy, such as had not been in all land of Egypt since it became a nation.
The hail struck in all land of Egypt all that was in field, from human to beast; hail struck every plant of field and shattered every tree of field.
Only in land of Goshen, where children of Israel were, there was no hail.
Pharaoh sent and called Moses and Aaron and said to them, I have sinned/misaligned this time. YHWH is righteous/just, and I and my people are wicked.
Plead to YHWH, for enough of Divine thunders and hail; I will send you away, and you shall no longer stay.
Moses said to him, As I go out from city, I will spread my palms to YHWH; thunder will cease, and hail will be no more, so you may know earth belongs to YHWH.
But as for you and your servants, I know you do not yet fear/revere YHWH Divine.
The flax and barley were struck, for barley was in ear and flax in bud.
But wheat and spelt were not struck, for they are late.
Moses went out from Pharaoh, from city, and spread his palms to YHWH; thunder and hail ceased, and rain was not poured on earth.
Pharaoh saw that rain, hail, and thunder ceased; he sinned/misaligned again and made his heart heavy, he and his servants.
Heart of Pharaoh was strong/hardened, and he did not send the children of Israel, as YHWH had spoken by hand of Moses.

translation_notes: The hail warning allows Egyptians/servants who fear YHWH's word to seek shelter; this prevents ethnic flattening.
Pharaoh's confession is real speech but not yet deep turning; relief again leads to renewed hardening.
Earth belongs to YHWH is the theological claim against Pharaoh's control.
safety_note: The signs are not anti-Egyptian ethnicity. Some of Pharaoh's own servants heed YHWH's word and protect life. The target remains hardened oppressive power.
